Method.desc: Merges configuration values into the spoon. Accepts any of the public variables (invertVertical, invertHorizontal, smoothness).
Method.doc:
Merges configuration values into the spoon. Accepts any of the public variables (invertVertical, invertHorizontal, smoothness). smoothness is clamped to [0, 10] and rounded to the nearest integer.

Parameters:
 * configuration - a table of configuration values to merge into the spoon

Returns:
 * self
Method.name: configure
parameters: * configuration - a table of configuration values to merge into the spoon

Method.desc: Installs the scroll-event tap. Errors loudly if Hammerspoon does not have Accessibility permission.
Method.doc:
Installs the scroll-event tap. Errors loudly if Hammerspoon does not have Accessibility permission, since hs.eventtap silently fails without it. Idempotent - calling start() again replaces the tap.

Returns:
 * self
Method.name: start

Method.desc: Stops the event tap and cancels any in-flight smoothing animation.
Method.doc:
Stops the event tap and cancels any in-flight smoothing animation.

Returns:
 * self
Method.name: stop

Method.desc: Toggles the Spoon on/off and shows a brief hs.alert banner.
Method.doc: Toggles the Spoon on/off and shows a brief hs.alert banner so the user can tell which state they're in without checking the console. Useful as a hotkey binding for apps where wheel-direction inversion or smoothing would get in the way (games, drawing apps).
Method.name: toggle

Method.desc: Binds (or rebinds) keyboard shortcuts. The mapping table accepts a toggle key with a {mods, key} pair compatible with hs.hotkey.bindSpec.
Method.doc:
Binds (or rebinds) keyboard shortcuts. The mapping table accepts a toggle key with a {mods, key} pair compatible with hs.hotkey.bindSpec. Calling bindHotkeys again clears prior bindings first.

Parameters:
 * mapping - table like { toggle = {{"ctrl","cmd"}, "m"} }

Returns:
 * self
Method.name: bindHotkeys

Variable.desc: If true (default), the vertical mouse-wheel direction is flipped.
Variable.doc: If true (default), the vertical mouse-wheel direction is flipped. Affects only discrete (non-continuous) scroll events - trackpads and Magic Mouse are unaffected.
Variable.name: invertVertical

Variable.desc: If true (default), the horizontal (tilt-wheel) direction is flipped.
Variable.doc: If true (default), the horizontal (tilt-wheel) direction is flipped. Affects only discrete scroll events.
Variable.name: invertHorizontal

Variable.desc: Smoothing feel for discrete wheel ticks, integer in [0, 20]. 0 (default) means passthrough.
Variable.doc: Smoothing feel for discrete wheel ticks, integer in [0, 20]. 0 (default) means passthrough - only inversion applies. Higher values primarily tune the momentum tail length and per-tick acceleration multiplier; the per-tick base buffer stays small, so single isolated clicks remain modest at any grade. Mid grades (5-10) feel subtle; 10-15 approximates MMF; 15-20 leans further into long glides. Magnitude on rapid scrolling comes from the MMF-style two-level acceleration (consecutive ticks within ~130 ms boost the buffer, and 3+ accumulated swipes engage a further fast-scroll multiplier). Read live at each tick, so it can be retuned via :configure({smoothness=N}) without restarting the spoon.
Variable.name: smoothness

Variable.desc: Logger object used within the Spoon. Set its level to control verbosity.
Variable.doc: Logger object used within the Spoon. Set its level (e.g. spoon.MouseScrollTweaks.logger.setLogLevel("debug")) to see the decision trace.
Variable.name: logger

desc: Tweaks for traditional mouse wheels on macOS, without touching the trackpad or Magic Mouse.
doc:
Tweaks for traditional mouse wheels on macOS, without touching the trackpad or Magic Mouse:

1. Per-axis direction inversion - flip the vertical and/or horizontal wheel direction independently of the system "Natural scrolling" toggle (which on macOS ties trackpad and mouse-wheel direction together).
2. Smoothness - interpolate each discrete wheel tick into a short sequence of small continuous pixel events, exposed as a grade from 0 (off / passthrough) to 10 (longest, smoothest glide).

Trackpads and Magic Mouse produce continuous scroll events and are passed through untouched, distinguished via the scrollWheelEventIsContinuous property.
name: MouseScrollTweaks
